data.summary: Self-taught full-stack engineer who left a formal CS programme due to financial constraints and spent the next two years building a production multi-tenant SaaS platform (620 000+ lines of code, 331 REST endpoints) using Java 17, Spring Boot 3.5, React 19, TypeScript, and AWS — while formalising knowledge in security, networking, and Java through Google and LinkedIn certifications in parallel. Comfortable across the full SDLC: schema design, distributed service architecture, CI/CD, and live AWS operations. Ready to bring that end-to-end delivery depth to JUMO's banking-as-a-service platform.

experience.company: FIN Financial Management System — Sthwalo Holdings (Pty) Ltd
experience.note: Development began April 2024 following a structured self-study period (May 2023 – March 2024) covering Java, Spring Boot, JavaScript, SQL, and cybersecurity through LinkedIn Learning and Google Coursera certifications. FIN went live in production within the same development period.
bullets: Designed, built, and shipped a production multi-tenant SaaS platform with 620 000+ lines of code, 331 REST API endpoints across 30 Spring Boot controllers, 90 service classes, and 54 JPA repositories — delivered solo in approximately 2 years

Architected a clean layered system (Controller → Service → Repository → Entity) applying Repository, DTO, Service Layer, and AOP design patterns — including a PlanEnforcementAspect that gates subscription features without coupling to business logic

Built a SARS-compliant double-entry accounting engine (Java 17 / Spring Boot 3.5 / PostgreSQL) that generates balanced GL journal entries automatically from every financial event — zero manual debit/credit decisions for the end user

Implemented a 39-endpoint payroll module covering SARS tax bracket calculations, UIF and SDL contributions, IRP5-aligned payslip PDF generation via Apache PdfBox, and automatic GL posting — no manual journal entry required post-payroll

Designed a 62-table multi-tenant PostgreSQL schema with full tenant isolation via company_id, evolved safely across a live RDS instance using 18 versioned Flyway migrations including PostgreSQL functions, triggers, and index strategies

Developed a bank statement processing pipeline with spatial coordinate extraction (SpatialDocumentTextExtractor) to parse PDFs from 4 South African banks regardless of column layout, feeding a rules-based TransactionClassificationEngine for automatic GL account posting

Delivered a React 19 / TypeScript 5 / Vite frontend with 52 fully typed components covering Balance Sheet, Income Statement, Cash Flow, Trial Balance, Payroll, Budgeting, Asset Register, VAT, and Audit Trail — mobile-responsive at 5 breakpoints (600px–900px)
Integrated live PayFast recurring subscription billing: registration via form POST, IPN webhook confirmation, plan upgrade pro-rata charging — operating in live mode with real transactions

Operated full AWS infrastructure in af-south-1: EC2 (systemd-managed JAR), RDS PostgreSQL (encrypted, managed), S3 (artifact storage and daily pg_dump backups with 30-day retention), Secrets Manager (runtime credential injection), SSM Session Manager (no SSH key exposure)

Designed and maintained 11 GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows covering build/test/deploy, parallel lint and compile, Docker image build and push to GHCR, CodeQL SAST, GitGuardian secret scanning, Gradle lock file integrity validation, and PR gate checks

Engineered security controls aligned with production standards: JWT via HttpOnly cookies with refresh token rotation, BCrypt password hashing, HSTS + TLS via Nginx + Certbot, strict CSP with violation reporting, request-level rate limiting, CORS restricted to declared origins, and zero credentials in codebase via AWS Secrets Manager

Generated a 20-service financial reporting suite producing Balance Sheet, Income Statement, Cash Flow (direct and indirect), Trial Balance, Aged Receivables/Payables, Management Accounts, and Payroll Analysis — all exportable as PDF
Remediated a git history credential exposure across 496 commits using git filter-repo, force-pushed a clean history with zero code loss and zero production downtime

Implemented an OCR document processing pipeline (ImageDocumentTextExtractor, OcrSpatialExtractor) enabling mobile-captured supplier invoices and PDF uploads to be parsed and auto-populated into document records without manual re-entry

Applied security knowledge formalised through the Google Cybersecurity Specialization directly to production: threat modelling informed the JWT + HttpOnly cookie auth design, network security fundamentals shaped CORS and CSP configuration, and asset/vulnerability awareness drove the decision to use AWS Secrets Manager over environment variables for all credentials

education.institution: IU International University of Applied Sciences
education.honors: Completed first-semester coursework. Left formal programme due to financial constraints and immediately resumed learning through hands-on project delivery and self-directed study in Java, Spring Boot, data structures, and software architecture. Full production system (FIN) built independently in the two years following.

technical: Java 17 (collections, exception handling, JDBC, OOP — formally certified)
SQL (production use + Google Cybersecurity cert: Linux and SQL module)
REST API design (331 endpoints in production)
JavaScript (certified, production use in TypeScript and React)
Linux CLI (Google Cybersecurity cert: Tools of the Trade)
Network security fundamentals (Google Cybersecurity cert: Connect and Protect)
Threat and vulnerability analysis (Google Cybersecurity cert: Assets, Threats, and Vulnerabilities)
Security incident detection and response (Google Cybersecurity cert: Sound the Alarm)
Security risk management frameworks (Google Cybersecurity cert: Play It Safe)
soft: End-to-end ownership across the full SDLC
Autonomous problem-solving under real production constraints
Systems thinking — design decisions traced through to operational impact
Stakeholder communication (translating accounting complexity into engineering requirements and vice versa)
Risk anticipation and proactive escalation before cost or timeline ballooning
Continuous formal learning alongside active delivery (17 certifications completed while building FIN)
Attention to detail — financial domain requires precision by design

certifications.name: Sound the Alarm: Detection and Response
Tools of the Trade: Linux and SQL
Connect and Protect: Networks and Network Security
Play It Safe: Manage Security Risks
Java Essential Training: Objects and APIs
Java Essential Training: Syntax and Structure

projects.name: FIN — Multi-Tenant Financial Management SaaS Platform
projects.description: End-to-end SaaS platform purpose-built for SARS compliance in South African small businesses. Automates the full accounting layer — payroll, invoicing, bank reconciliation, VAT, asset depreciation, budgeting, and financial reporting — so business owners produce audit-ready financials without accounting knowledge. Live in production with real paying customers on a PayFast recurring billing integration.
bullets: Designed a 62-table multi-tenant PostgreSQL schema covering GL, payroll, assets, budgeting, sales, banking, VAT, documents, and audit — all with tenant isolation via company_id

Built a double-entry GL engine that generates balanced journal entries automatically for every financial event (invoices, payroll, bank transactions, asset depreciation, VAT settlements) — the user never constructs a debit or credit

Delivered the highest-complexity module: 39-endpoint SARS-compliant payroll with PAYE tax bracket evaluation, UIF (1%/1%) and SDL (1%) calculation, PDF payslip generation, SMTP payslip distribution, and automatic GL posting via PayrollPostingService

Engineered a bank statement import pipeline supporting Absa, FNB, Capitec, and ServiceFee PDF formats using spatial coordinate-based text extraction — enabling reliable parsing across inconsistent column layouts

Implemented a customer invoicing module that posts AR (DR Trade Debtors / CR Revenue / CR VAT Output) and discount entries (DR Discount Allowed / CR Trade Debtors) automatically, with separate code paths for VAT-registered and non-VAT companies

Shipped 20 financial reporting services producing SARS-ready Balance Sheet, Income Statement, Cash Flow (direct + indirect method), Trial Balance, Aged Receivables/Payables, and Payroll Analysis — all rendered to PDF via Apache PdfBox

Set up 11 GitHub Actions workflows covering deploy pipeline (bootJar → S3 → SSM pull → systemd restart → health check), parallel lint/compile, CodeQL SAST, GitGuardian secret scan, Docker image build to GHCR, and Gradle lock file validation
Applied AOP (PlanEnforcementAspect) to enforce subscription plan feature gating across the entire service layer without a single if-check in business logic

Implemented JWT authentication with HttpOnly cookies and refresh token rotation, strict CSP, HSTS, rate-limiting interceptor, CORS origin restriction, and zero-credential codebase via AWS Secrets Manager

Safely evolved a live RDS schema with 18 Flyway migrations — including a full database recreation event where employee data (38+ fields per record) was extracted via SSM-executed SQL, backed to S3, and fully restored without UI import tooling
Built a fully mobile-responsive React 19 / TypeScript frontend (52 components, strict typing, 5 breakpoints) enabling payroll, invoicing, and financial reporting from a mobile phone on site

projects.name: Spatial Bank Statement Parser
projects.description: A coordinate-based PDF text extraction engine that parses South African bank statements from Absa, FNB, Capitec, and a service-fee format — resolving the challenge that these PDFs do not share a consistent column layout across institutions. Feeds parsed transactions into a rules-based classification engine for automatic GL account posting.
bullets: Built SpatialDocumentTextExtractor to locate and group text tokens by page coordinates rather than line order — enabling layout-agnostic parsing across 4 distinct bank PDF formats

Designed a TransactionClassificationEngine that applies company-defined and template rules to suggest GL account postings for each imported transaction, reducing administrator classification effort to review-and-confirm
Integrated the pipeline into the bank reconciliation module, enabling statement import, classification, and GL posting in a single flow without manual re-entry of transaction data

projects.name: SARS-Compliant Payroll Engine
projects.description: The largest single module in FIN — 39 REST endpoints covering the full payroll lifecycle from employee setup through payslip distribution and GL posting. Implements SARS tax bracket evaluation, UIF and SDL contribution rules, IRP5-aligned data structures, PDF payslip generation, and SMTP email distribution.
bullets: Implemented SARS PAYE tax bracket lookup and marginal rate calculation, UIF (1% employee / 1% employer) and SDL (1% employer) contribution rules — all configurable per tax year
Generated IRP5-aligned PDF payslips via Apache PdfBox and distributed them directly to employee email addresses via SMTP on payroll run completion

Designed PayrollPostingService to automatically create balanced GL journal entries for each payroll period — salary expense, PAYE payable, UIF payable, SDL payable, and net pay — eliminating all manual journal construction
Built payroll period management, employee tax profile, and payroll history endpoints to support full payroll audit trail requirements
